business.headline: The hidden salary leak Gen Z didn't notice until bank balances started shrinkingYoung salaried Indians are discovering that small app subscriptions are quietly eating into their monthly income. The online debate has sharpened focus on how auto-debits and convenience spending can distort budgeting.

Is E20 petrol quietly increasing your monthly fuel bill? 5 in 10 owners say yesA LocalCircles survey says many owners of older petrol vehicles have seen lower mileage after the E20 rollout. The findings sharpen consumer concerns over fuel costs, repairs and the shift towards higher ethanol blends.

Remote work is back in focus: Will it redefine how India chooses homes?Flexible work is again influencing how Indian homebuyers assess where to live. The shift could move demand beyond office hubs towards space, practicality and quality of life.

8th Pay Commission: Pension choice for government employees in coming monthsAt present, most central government employees recruited after January 1, 2004, fall under the National Pension System, which is contribution-based and linked to market performance.

Is RBI really supposed to defend the rupee amid ongoing crisis?Handcrafted ByOur Editors Every time the rupee falls, panic erupts across television studios, political circles and financial markets. But what if the RBI was never meant to save the rupee in the first place?

NSE, BSE closed today for Bakri Id. Here's when stock markets reopenMarkets shut on 28 May for Bakri Id, with most trading segments closed. The break has left some investors checking timings, while commodity derivatives reopen in the evening and regular trading returns on 29 May.

Why do banks prefer lending to women more than men?For years, loans and borrowing have largely been seen as a man's domain. But banks are quietly noticing something interesting: women borrowers are often proving to be more reliable when it comes to repaying loans. So, are lenders beginning to trust women more with credit?

Need fairness: Supreme Court quashes CCI's Rs 202 crore penalty on AmazonThe Supreme Court set aside the CCI's Rs 202 crore penalty on Amazon and quashed orders suspending its Future Coupons deal. The ruling ends a five-year legal battle and underscores that regulators must act with legality, fairness and reasoned decision-making.

Hiranandani says India's affordable housing crisis is getting worse. Here's proofFor many aspiring homeowners, the biggest challenge today is no longer deciding where to buy a house, it is finding one that fits the budget. As affordable housing supply drops, experts warn that the problem is becoming harder to ignore.

US delegation to visit India from June 1–4 to finalise interim trade pactThe discussions are expected to focus on important areas such as market access, non-tariff measures, customs and trade facilitation, investment promotion and economic security alignment.

Sensex slips 142 points, Nifty ends flat as auto and metal stocks support marketThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 141.90 points, or 0.19%, to close at 75,867.80. Meanwhile, the NSE Nifty50 slipped just 6.55 points, or 0.03%, to settle at 23,907.15.

Air India to cut domestic flights by 22% as Iran war pushes fuel costs higherThe cuts will affect Air India's domestic operations for June and July, while decisions for August are yet to be finalised.

HDFC Bank responds to Rs 45 crore payment allegations, denies 'wrongdoing'HDFC Bank shares fell sharply during Wednesday's trading session. The stock dropped nearly 2.6%, trading at Rs 758.75 on the NSE around 1:29 pm, down Rs 20.15.

HDFC Bank shares drop 2% on report of probe into Rs 45-crore interest paymentsThe stock dropped nearly 2.6% during the trading session after the report surfaced. As per NSE data cited in the report, HDFC Bank shares were trading at Rs 758.75, down Rs 20.15 or 2.59%, around 1:29 pm.

8th Pay Commission: Why a union wants 5 different fitment factors instead of oneThe Indian Railway Technical Supervisors' Association (IRTSA) has urged the 8th Pay Commission to replace a single fitment factor with five graded multipliers. The demand shifts the discussion from overall salary hikes to how different levels should be paid.

Air India trims domestic flights by 15–20% during July–August as fuel costs soarIn a statement, an Air India spokesperson said the airline has temporarily adjusted some domestic services after earlier changes to international routes.

How AI helped Taiwan overtake India to become the world's 5th largest stock marketTaiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company is the world's largest contract chipmaker and plays a major role in producing advanced semiconductors used in AI systems.

Not a finding of fraud, dishonesty: Byju Raveendran on jail sentence in SingaporeResponding to the developments, Raveendran said the matter has been misunderstood and is part of ongoing settlement talks with lenders and stakeholders.

From Messi to mounting lawsuits: Inside the collapse of Byju's empireA Singapore court sentenced Byju Raveendran to six months in jail for contempt over asset-related orders. The ruling sharpens focus on Byju's steep fall from a $22 billion edtech darling to a company facing debt, lawsuits and insolvency proceedings.

Air India, BigBasket, Tata Electronics: Inside Tata's key boardroom discussionsTata Sons' latest board meeting stayed calm as operating chiefs answered questions on Air India, BigBasket and other businesses. The discussions showed tighter scrutiny of profits, capital allocation and the group's long-term strategic bets.

entertainment.headline: Who is MasterChef Pankaj Bhadouria? India's recipe queen battling breast cancer Masterchef Pankaj Bhadouria has revealed that she has been diagnosed with breast cancer. The update has renewed attention on the MasterChef India Season 1 winner's journey from Lucknow teacher to celebrity chef.

Netflix this week: Kara, Rafa, Murder Mindfully Season 2 and more Netflix's latest weekly lineup includes a mix of sports documentaries, thrillers, reality dramas and international releases. From Dhanush's Kara to Rafael Nadal documentary Rafa, here's everything releasing on the platform this week.

11 years after Tamasha, Imtiaz Ali feels guilty that Ved inspired fans to quit jobs Imtiaz Ali said some viewers told him they quit their jobs after watching Tamasha. He said their stories leave him hopeful yet burdened by the risks families take for creative dreams.

It worked till it didn't: Pooja Bhatt gets real about romancing magical Bobby Deol Pooja Bhatt has looked back on her past relationship with Bobby Deol with warmth and restraint. She said they grew in different directions and stressed dignity while speaking about former partners.

In Bengali filmmaker Anik Dutta's alleged suicide note, a message for daughter Police are examining a handwritten note addressed to Anik Dutta's daughter after his death in Kolkata. The alleged note had a message for the filmmaker's daughter.

MasterChef Pankaj Bhadouria diagnosed with breast cancer, seeks prayers MasterChef India winner Pankaj Bhadouria has said she has been diagnosed with breast cancer and shared hospital updates online. She asked followers, whom she called extended family, to keep her in their prayers.

Kattalan review: Antony Varghese's film is all style, little substanceRelease Date:Thu May 28, 2026 Kattalan movie review: Set in the world of Marco, Kattalan presents a stylised tale of elephant tusk smugglers caught in a power struggle over the cartel and the forest that fuels their illegal trade.

You've achieved: Rajinikanth congratulates Karuppu director over film's success Rajinikanth called RJ Balaji after watching Karuppu and praised the film's success. The appreciation came as Suriya's fantasy drama crossed Rs 200 crore globally and revived box-office momentum for Tamil cinema.

Kangana channels ordinary people's bravery in Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata's new glimpse Actor-politician Kangana Ranaut steps into the role of a nurse in her next, Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata. The film is inspired by the bravery shown during the 26/11 Mumbai attacks.

Exclusive: Obsession cast on love, fixation and fear in modern dating Curry Barker, Michael Johnston and Inde Navarrette discuss how Obsession turns romance into psychological horror, in an exclusive chat with India Today. They said the film draws its fear from emotional dependence, blurred boundaries and the line between love and obsession.

WATCH: Curry Barker, Michael Johnston, Inde Navarrette on healthy OBSESSION, love, OG Nikki's end In an exclusive chat with India Today's Bhavna Agarwal, Obsession stars Michael Johnston and Inde Navarrette, along with writer-director Curry Barker, break down the film's themes of love, obsession, and survival. From "don't show the shark" horror lessons to changing the ending after one powerful take— this one's a must-watch.

Sourav Ganguly biopic: Rajkummar Rao starts Kolkata shoot at Eden Gardens Rajkummar Rao has begun filming Sourav Ganguly's biopic across key Kolkata locations. The schedule traces Ganguly's early life while building the film's Bengali setting and cast.

Why Return of the Jungle director Vaibhav Kumaresh admires KJo, Anurag Kashyap Director Vaibhav Kumaresh, in an interview, shared that his upcoming animated feature film, Return of the Jungle, was made for Indian audiences and rooted in Indian culture. The movie is slated to release in theatres on May 29.

Shape of Momo director Tribeny Rai on patriarchy, identity, Northeast representation Ahead of The Shape of Momo's May 29 release, director Tribeny Rai has spoken about the film's women-led story and symbols. Her comments place the Nepali-language drama within wider debates on patriarchy, identity and representation.

Blast review: Big bang ideas that fizzle out before the final frameRelease Date:Thu May 28, 2026 Blast movie review: Director Subash K Raj's Blast, starring Preity Mukundhan, Arjun Sarja and Abhirami, is an interesting dark thriller with promising ideas. However, the film's predictable screenplay and long runtime keep it from reaching its full potential.

When SRK and Akshay tried to stop Honey Singh from spiralling: Drugs will finish you Honey Singh has said Shah Rukh Khan and Akshay Kumar repeatedly warned him that drugs would destroy him. He recalled the period when fame, addiction and poor choices pushed his career and personal life into crisis.

Salman ko ban karke dikhao: Rakhi Sawant lashes out at Farhan Akhtar over Don 3 row Rakhi Sawant has publicly backed Ranveer Singh as the FWICE sharpened its stand in the Don 3 dispute. While defending the actor, Rakhi claimed that everyone is jealous of Ranveer.

Samantha left blushing after fan surprises her with flowers on stage. Watch Samantha Ruth Prabhu was left blushing after a fan rushed onto the stage and offered her flowers during the Maa Inti Bangaaram trailer launch in Hyderabad. The actor's sweet reaction quickly won hearts online.

Why Allu Arjun, Ram Charan are promoting films in Bihar, MP like election campaigns While Bollywood stayed inside Mumbai studios, Allu Arjun and Ram Charan were busy turning Patna, Bhopal and Lucknow into fan carnivals.

general news.headline: CBI arrests Twisha Sharma's mother-in-law after court cancels anticipatory bailThe arrest came just hours after the Madhya Pradesh High Court cancelled the anticipatory bail granted to Twisha Sharma's mother-in-law Giribala Singh.

Siddaramaiah declines Congress's Rajya Sabha offer, picks state over national role  Live updatesShortly after resigning as Karnataka CM, Siddaramaiah broke his silence on the high command's decision, rejected a Rajya Sabha offer, defended Congress guarantees and delivered an emotional farewell packed with political messaging and sharp attacks.

I take responsibility: Dharmendra Pradhan breaks silence on CBSE OSM controversyUnion Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan addressed the controversy over CBSE's On-Screen Marking system, taking responsibility for OSM discrepancies and student inconvenience, and assuring action if irregularities are found.

The two worlds of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: When respect stops at the boundary ropeIPL 2026 Eliminator: Off the field, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is all grace and humility, seeking blessings from legends before battle. On it, he is something else entirely: a fifteen-year-old phenomenon for whom respect ends precisely where the boundary rope begins.

Siddaramiah's quiet revenge on DKS and Rahul GandhiJust before stepping down as the Chief Minister of Karnataka, Siddaramaiah decided to accept a caste census report, pending since November 2025. With this, he is leaving a ticking bomb for his successor, DK Shivakumar, and the Congress's de facto chief, Rahul Gandhi.

Delhi, Noida on orange alert; heavy rain and duststorm warning for GurugramAfter days of relentless heat and unusually warm nights, a weather shift is expected in the coming hours. Here are the places that will enjoy a cooler evening today.

Live: Siddaramaiah leaves for Delhi, set to meet Rahul Gandhi after resignationKarnataka CM News Live Updates: Siddaramaiah has resigned as the Chief Minister of Karnataka after informing his Cabinet colleagues of his decision during a breakfast meeting hosted at his official residence earlier in the day. He later held a joint press conference with Deputy Chief Minister DK Shivakumar amid dramatic political developments in the state. Karnataka witnessed a major political transition on Thursday as Siddaramaiah stepped down from the Chief Minister's post, paving the way for Deputy Chief Minister DK Shivakumar to take over the top job. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for the latest live updates from Karnataka.

NEET-UG re-test: PMO steps in, to monitor paper setting to final delivery processThe Centre has tightened oversight of NEET 2026 after the paper leak controversy, with PMO officials set to closely monitor everything from paper setting and printing to transportation and delivery at exam centres. The decision was taken during a high-level meeting chaired at Defence Minister Rajnath Singh's residence in Delhi.

How greed brought down Byju Raveendran's empire from $22 billion to zeroOnce valued at $22 billion, Byju's is now buried under debt, lawsuits, unpaid dues, and careers that are still not recovered. Founder Byju Raveendran has been sentenced in absentia to jail in Singapore. Byju's collapse has become India's biggest cautionary tale on startup greed and unchecked ambition.

After firing 8,000 employees, Meta may enter cloud computing spaceMeta may soon take on Amazon Web Services (AWS). Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg has claimed that it could join the cloud computing market as it continues to get requests for selling compute.

After Iran showdown, Trump threatens to blow up Oman for this reasonDonald Trump warns that no country will control the Strait of Hormuz. His statement stuns many because Oman has long been one of Washington's closest and oldest Gulf partners.

Rajasthan woman, 22, pregnant with sixth child for another son; doctor raises alarmThe video was shared by Dr Pragya Tomar, a gynaecologist at Spandan Hospital in Baraut, who questioned the toll that repeated pregnancies can take on a woman's health when driven by a preference for male children.

IIT, IAS, MBA: The hidden dowry rate card still haunting Indian marriagesHandcrafted ByOur Editors Dowry may be illegal in India, but unofficial "rates" linked to education and jobs still survive. From IAS officers to IIT graduates, groom value often rises with degrees and salaries. This article explores dowry deaths, academic research, and why families are questioning a system that still monetises educated men.

22-year-old Indian student from Gujarat stabbed to death in CanadaPolice said Vidhi Megha was attacked in broad daylight and sustained fatal stab injuries. Local residents rushed her to a nearby hospital following the incident, but doctors declared her dead on arrival.

Indian tourists perform garba near aircraft at Vietnam airport. Video sparks outrageA video showed Indian tourists performing garba beside a VietJet Air aircraft at an airport in Vietnam. The clip drew mixed reactions online over cultural expression, travel etiquette and behaviour in regulated spaces.

Rs 2.24 crore and counting: Cash stash found buried near TMC leader's farmhouseBengal police have arrested TMC leader Dipankar Bhattacharya and unearthed cash and gold buried in a jute field behind his residence. The recovery, which crossed Rs 2.24 crore, has triggered a political row.

Shakti Shalini's dark twist out as Aneet Padda wraps up film set in Bengali folkloreShakti Shalini reveals Aneet Padda in a double role as Shakti and Shalini, whose clash drives the film. The folklore-rooted horror comedy expands Maddock Films' universe after completing its final schedule.

Why Chandrayaan-2's Moon water discovery is an Indian gift to the worldChandrayaan-2 orbiter data has revealed signs of subsurface water-ice in shadowed craters near the Moon's south pole. The finding sharpens the strategic importance of the polar region for future missions, lunar bases and resource use.

Delhi man made 100 AI nude photos, videos of influencer after rejection, arrestedWhat began as religious chats on Instagram allegedly spiralled into a disturbing AI-driven revenge campaign involving fake accounts, nude videos and relentless online humiliation targeting an Ahmedabad influencer and her mother.

Siddaramaiah resigns, paves way for DK Shivakumar. What's next for him?Among the biggest OBC leaders of the Congress, Siddaramaiah resigned as the Chief Minister of Karnataka after months of tug-of-war with his bete noire, DK Shivakumar, over the top post.

Ajit Doval stresses safe Hormuz trade route, calls for special focus on West AsiaAjit Doval warned in Moscow that tensions in West Asia could disrupt trade through the Strait of Hormuz and the Red Sea. He also called for tougher action on terrorism and reforms to global security institutions.

We voted here for years: Illegal migrants crowd Bengal border amid crackdownMany migrants at the Bengal border said they had worked as domestic helpers, labourers and rag-pickers in Kolkata's outskirts before being forced to vacate rented homes. Several admitted crossing illegally through agents, while others said they were brought to India as children and barely remembered Bangladesh.

Giribala Singh left no stone unturned to malign Twisha's character: CBI to courtThe CBI told the high court that Twisha Sharma had suffered injuries, but no explanation had been given by her in-laws regarding how those injuries were caused. The submissions came days after questions were raised over injury marks mentioned in Twisha's post-mortem report.

sports.headline: Yashasvi Jaiswal must leave RR to break out of Sooryavanshi's shadow: Rayudu Ambati Rayudu has said Yashasvi Jaiswal should leave Rajasthan Royals rather than remain in Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's shadow. Jaiswal has struggled for consistency this season with the bat as Sooryavanshi has gone ahead of him in terms of striking.

India Women turn training into water balloon war in fun session: Video ENG vs IND: India Women mixed fun with preparation ahead of the England T20Is, turning a unique water-balloon catching drill into a hilarious splash-filled battle during training.

Esha Singh wins Munich gold with 25m pistol world record India's Esha Singh scripted history at the ISSF World Cup in Munich after clinching gold in the women's 25m pistol with a world record score. The 21-year-old outshot an elite field that included Olympic champion Yang Ji-in.

Day 5 Live Updates: Sinner wins 2nd set vs Cerundolo, Osaka beats Vekic French Open Day 5 Live Updates: Naomi Osaka has beaten Donna Vekic and has stormed into the third round. In men's singles, Jannik Sinner is locking horns with Juan Manuel Cerndolo on Centre Court. Later in the day, Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff, and Ben Shelton will also be in action.

India eye one last push against England as Women's T20 World Cup nears India Women will face England Women in a three-match T20I series before the 2026 Women's T20 World Cup. The tour gives India a final chance to test combinations, while England balance momentum with injury concerns.

Ashwin urges Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's parents to protect starboy from evil eye IPL 2026: Ravichandran Ashwin was left stunned by Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's maturity after the RR teenager's brutal Eliminator knock against SRH. The former India spinner even joked that the 15-year-old's parents should protect him from the "evil eye".

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi humbled by Sachin Tendulkar's praise: It motivates me IPL 2026, RR vs SRH: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi said Sachin Tendulkar's praise was a huge honour and a motivating moment after his blistering 97 off 29 balls against Sunrisers Hyderabad powered Rajasthan Royals into the Qualifier 2.

Day 5 schedule: Jannik Sinner, Aryna Sabalenka aim for more dominance Day 5 of the French Open 2026 features a packed schedule, with top seeds Jannik Sinner and Aryna Sabalenka continuing their campaigns. Among Indians, Anirudh Chandrasekar will be in action in men's doubles while Yuki Bhambri will play mixed doubles.

The two worlds of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: When respect stops at the boundary rope IPL 2026 Eliminator: Off the field, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is all grace and humility, seeking blessings from legends before battle. On it, he is something else entirely: a fifteen-year-old phenomenon for whom respect ends precisely where the boundary rope begins.

Kavya Maran congratulates Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after SRH's Eliminator exit IPL 2026: Kavya Maran's emotional reactions during SRH's Eliminator loss went viral online, but the SRH owner still made sure to congratulate Rajasthan Royals teenager Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after his stunning knock. The 15-year-old's 97 off 29 balls knocked Hyderabad out of the title race.

Deja vu strikes: Praggnanandhaa shocks Carlsen again in Norway Chess clash Indian Grandmaster R. Praggnanandhaa stunned World No. 1 Magnus Carlsen again at Norway Chess 2026 in Oslo, continuing a growing rivalry after his breakthrough win over Carlsen at the same event in 2024.

Djokovic blasts Paris crowd after boos during tense Roland Garros clashFrench Open 2026: Novak Djokovic slammed sections of the Roland Garros crowd after being booed during his win over Frenchman Valentin Royer. The Serbian accused the Paris spectators of showing "no respect" as he battled into the third round.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India debut loading? Captain Suryakumar already a fanIPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's fearless batting is now turning into a genuine India selection conversation. After the 15-year-old's breathtaking Eliminator blitz against SRH, India T20 captain Suryakumar Yadav joined the growing list of cricket stars praising the RR teenager.

IPL Play of the Day: Rajasthan giveth, Royals taketh awayIPL 2026: SunRisers Hyderabad were blown out of the park in the Eliminator of the Indian Premier League. Playing against Rajasthan Royals, SRH put up a sorry show at the Mullanpur Stadium on Wednesday.

Don't fast-track Sooryavanshi's India selection: Ex-batter issues warningAakash Chopra has urged selectors not to fast-track Vaibhav Sooryavanshi into the India side after his latest IPL blitz. Sooryavanshi was the star of the show as RR defeated SRH in Mullanpur.

Chris Gayle crowns Vaibhav 'new six machine' after breaking iconic IPL recordUniverse Boss Chris Gayle crowned Vaibhav Sooryavanshi cricket's "new six machine" after the Rajasthan Royals teenager shattered his iconic IPL record for most sixes in a single season with a breathtaking display against Sunrisers Hyderabad in the Eliminator.

Coach quits via text message, leaves Spanish tennis star stunned at French OpenSpain's Alejandro Davidovich Fokina was left stunned at the French Open after coach Mariano Puerta suddenly quit through a text message and flew to Miami without informing anyone on the team.

Baptiste suffers horrific knee injury at French Open, forced off on wheelchairHailey Baptiste retired from her French Open second-round match after collapsing and injuring her left knee against Wang Xinyu. The distressing exit halted the best run of her career and raised fears of a long lay-off.

Fastest hundred missed, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi manifests IPL glory for RRVaibhav Sooryavanshi missed out on the fastest hundred in IPL history by the narrowest of margins, but the Rajasthan Royals teenager made it clear that personal milestones are secondary as he remains focused on helping the franchise win its second IPL title.

Archer takes dig at PBKS after RR reach Qualifier 2: Don't want Punjab's fateJofra Archer took a dig at Punjab Kings, saying that Rajasthan Royals don't want to get ahead of themselves and suffer the same fate as Shreyas Iyer and Co. PBKS crashed out of the play-offs race after a poor run of form in the second half of the campaign.

Kavya Maran in tears as SRH crash in IPL Eliminator vs Rajasthan RoyalsSunRisers Hyderabad owner Kavya Maran was nearly reduced to tears as her side crashed in the Eliminator of the Indian Premier League 2026.

Sooryavanshi 97 sends SunRisers home, Rajasthan closer to finalVaibhav Sooryavanshi's breathtaking 97 off 29 balls, laced with 12 sixes, set up Rajasthan Royals' 47-run win over SunRisers Hyderabad in the Eliminator in Mullanpur on Wednesday. RR will face Gujarat Titans on Friday in Qualifier 2.

Novak Djokovic matches Roger Federer's Open Era Grand Slam record at Roland GarrosFrench Open 2026: Novak Djokovic beat Valentin Royer in four sets after the Frenchman stole the third-set tie-break on Chatrier. The win kept his pursuit of a 25th Grand Slam alive and exposed the resistance home support can still create.

Virat Kohli meets 2008 U19 World Cup teammate, daughter before IPL 2026 finalVirat Kohli stepped off the team bus to meet former Under-19 teammate Taruwar Kohli and his daughter before the IPL 2026 final. The reunion, shared by Taruwar online, underlined a bond that survived sharply different careers and Kohli's rise to superstardom.

IOC mourns Randhir Singh's death, Olympic flag to fly at half-mast for three daysThe International Olympic Committee (IOC) on Wednesday mourned the death of veteran Indian sports administrator Randhir Singh, with the Olympic flag being flown at half-mast for three days at its headquarters here as a mark of respect.

Yuliia Starodubtseva delivers French Open shock, sends 2nd seed Elena Rybakina packingFrench Open 2026: Yuliia Starodubtseva beat second seed Elena Rybakina in a tense French Open second-round thriller. The upset blows open the women's draw and marks the biggest win of the Ukrainian's career.

Sanskari before Shikari: Watch Sooryavanshi's viral Gavaskar momentSRH vs RR, IPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's incredible batting was not the only thing that caught the eye on Wednesday. Ahead of the match vs SRH, Vaibhav sparked some hilarious reactions as he went to touch the feet of Sunil Gavaskar.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi foils SRH's Plan A, B, and C in IPL Eliminator15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi got off to a flyer against SunRisers Hyderabad in the IPL Eliminator on Wednesday. Vaibhav reached his fifty in just 16 balls, foiling all plans set by SRH in today's game.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi stands frozen on pitch after missing fastest IPL centuryIPL 2026 Eliminator, SRH vs RR: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi missed the record for the fastest century in the tournament when he was dismissed for 97 off 29 balls. The 15-year-old stood frozen in the middle, unable to believe his luck after he threw it away in the eighth over of the match in Mullanpur on Wednesday.

Vaibhav eclipses Chris Gayle, sets new IPL record for most sixes in a seasonVaibhav Sooryavanshi rewrote the IPL record books on Wednesday after breaking Chris Gayle's record for the most sixes in a single season during Rajasthan Royals' Eliminator clash against Sunrisers Hyderabad.

I had control over the game: Iga Swiatek cruises into French Open third roundIga Swiatek beat Sara Bejlek 6-2, 6-3 to reach the Roland-Garros third round. The score was tidy, but 38 unforced errors and a shaky serve showed there is still work to do in Paris.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
